raw_consumer_statement:
I searched online for movers XX/XX/year>. XXXX XXXX obtained my information & contacted me. They convinced me they were worth more because they would load everything for me. I WAS REASSURED MULTIPLE TIMES that THIS WAS A TOTAL CHARGE with NOTHING ADDITIONAL! Even tho I disclosed ( ie ) I had a stack of pictures & TVs. Nothing was said that I had to box everything, etc. I was told the price was a butterfly special offered by the government and the discount would be going away fast The caller would not quit talking-just kept telling me to just scroll to the bottom & sign-reassuring me that I could change arrangements any time. When I actually had a chance to read the disclosures, I found completely different information than I was told-including a no refund deposit. 

When the estimate was sent to me for signature, I was pushed to sign it - I was told that there was a " butterfly discount '' being applied and it was going to be gone soon so I didn't have time to waste. Also, I WAS REASSURED MULTIPLE TIMES that THIS WAS A TOTAL CHARGE and there would be NO ADDITIONAL CHARGES INCLUDING NO TAX!!!! 

Upon receiving & reading the moving company " rules '', I saw that " we pack everything '' was false. I read that actually the company required all TV 's & pictures to be boxed/packed. I called XXXX MINUTES after reading the contract sent to me to question why I was told " we pack everything '' only to find that the contract emailed to me different. I requested a cancellation. I was refused. Upon reading their cancellation policy, I saw that it said you have XXXX hours to reply by email & request a cancellation. I followed this rule and sent an email within XXXX hour after placing the order. When I saw the charge was still on my XXXX Citicard statement, I filed a dispute. Approximately XXXX month later, Citicard emailed me that the moving company refused refund so they put the charge back on my card. I complained again - I could not believe I followed the moving company rules/requirements and when they illegally ( in my opinion ) would not provide a refund, Citicard did no support me as their customer/client. Citicard opened a XXXX dispute. Again, after approximately a month, the moving company refused to refund and Citicard charged me! I believe it must be because I'm an easier target for collection. I thought a major reason for using a credit card was to protect you from scams/fraud. I don't understand why Citicard is not doing that!
